body.title: agent_datacenter: comms:// URI resolver — address any mailbox or device
body.result: agent_datacenter/bus/router.py: Router class with send(address, envelope) + resolve(address). AddressError on unknown/malformed URI. comms:// scheme only.
body.description:
Implement the comms:// URI scheme router. Callers are topology-blind — they address devices by name; the bus handles routing.

Design decision (Akien 2026-04-27): Pub/sub falls out naturally from IMAP IDLE. comms:// is the addressing scheme; mailbox IDLE = subscription. No separate pub/sub layer needed.

Proposed shape:
- agent_datacenter/bus/router.py:
  class Router:
    def send(self, address: str, envelope: Envelope) -> None:
      # address form: 'comms://{mailbox_name}'
      # parse mailbox_name; call imap_server.append(mailbox_name, envelope)
    def resolve(self, address: str) -> str:
      # extract mailbox_name from comms:// URI
      # verify mailbox exists in imap_server.list_mailboxes(); raise AddressError if not
  comms://Shared  → publish to Shared mailbox (all IDLE subscribers get it = pub/sub)
  comms://CC.0    → addressed to CC.0 mailbox (direct)
  comms://igor-wild-0001 → addressed to Igor's mailbox

Affected files:
- ~/dev/src/agent_datacenter/agent_datacenter/bus/router.py

Design rules:
- oop-first: Router class, not module-level send function
- docs-in-code: Router docstring explains comms:// = IMAP APPEND; pub/sub via IDLE

Scope boundary:
IN: URI parsing, mailbox resolution, IMAP APPEND dispatch
OUT: IMAP IDLE subscription (that's the web server and inference device), bus self-healing (T-adc-bus-self-healing)

Test plan:
- Router.send('comms://CC.0', envelope) → imap_server.unseen_count('CC.0') == 1
- Router.send('comms://Shared', envelope) → imap_server.unseen_count('Shared') == 1
- Router.send('comms://nonexistent', envelope) → AddressError
- Router.resolve('comms://CC.0') == 'CC.0'
